action:
import pyautogui
import time

# Move to the browser menu (three vertical dots in the upper-right corner)
pyautogui.moveTo(1890, 70, duration=0.5)
pyautogui.click()

# Wait for the dropdown to appear
time.sleep(0.5)

# Move to the "Settings" option
pyautogui.moveTo(1800, 250, duration=0.5)
pyautogui.click()

time.sleep(2)  # Wait for the settings page to load
response:
To clean up tracking data from Amazon, you should clear the cookies and browsing data from your web browser. Here's the next step to take:

1. Open the browser settings to access the privacy and security options.

I'll provide the code to do this in Chromium.

```python
import pyautogui
import time

# Move to the browser menu (three vertical dots in the upper-right corner)
pyautogui.moveTo(1890, 70, duration=0.5)
pyautogui.click()

# Wait for the dropdown to appear
time.sleep(0.5)

# Move to the "Settings" option
pyautogui.moveTo(1800, 250, duration=0.5)
pyautogui.click()

time.sleep(2)  # Wait for the settings page to load
```

This will help you access the settings where you can clear cookies and browsing data.

action:
import pyautogui
import time

# Move to the browser menu (three vertical dots in the upper-right corner)
pyautogui.moveTo(1890, 70, duration=0.5)
pyautogui.click()

# Wait for the dropdown to appear
time.sleep(0.5)

# Move to the "Settings" option
pyautogui.moveTo(1800, 250, duration=0.5)
pyautogui.click()

time.sleep(2)  # Wait for the settings page to load

# Open the privacy and security settings
pyautogui.hotkey('ctrl', 'shift', 'delete')

time.sleep(1)  # Wait for the clear browsing data dialog to appear

It seems we're still on the Amazon search page. To manage browsing privacy, we need to clear cookies and site data in Chromium. Let's navigate to the settings page:

```python
import pyautogui
import time

# Move to the browser menu (three vertical dots in the upper-right corner)
pyautogui.moveTo(1560, 60, duration=0.5)
pyautogui.click()

# Wait for the dropdown to appear
time.sleep(0.5)

# Move to the "Settings" option
pyautogui.moveTo(1470, 200, duration=0.5)
pyautogui.click()

time.sleep(2)  # Wait for the settings page to load
```

This should open the settings page to proceed with clearing cookies.

action:
import pyautogui
import time

# Open a new tab to ensure we're starting fresh
pyautogui.hotkey('ctrl', 't')
time.sleep(1)

# Open the settings page directly
pyautogui.typewrite('chrome://settings/clearBrowserData\n', interval=0.1)
time.sleep(2)

# Press 'Tab' to navigate through options and 'Enter' to clear data
pyautogui.press('tab', presses=5, interval=0.2)
pyautogui.press('enter')
time.sleep(1)

# Confirm the action if prompted
pyautogui.press('enter')

import pyautogui
import time

# Move to the browser menu (three vertical dots in the upper-right corner)
pyautogui.moveTo(1560, 60, duration=0.5)
pyautogui.click()

# Wait for the dropdown to appear
time.sleep(0.5)

# Move to the "More tools" option
pyautogui.moveTo(1470, 260, duration=0.5)
pyautogui.click()

# Move to the "Clear browsing data" option
pyautogui.moveTo(1470, 400, duration=0.5)
pyautogui.click()

time.sleep(2)  # Wait for the clear data dialog to appear

# Press 'Enter' to clear data
pyautogui.press('enter')
time.sleep(1)
response:
Since the screen hasn't changed, let's try a direct approach to open the settings using the menu. Here's the plan to clear cookies and browsing data:

```python
import pyautogui
import time

# Move to the browser menu (three vertical dots in the upper-right corner)
pyautogui.moveTo(1560, 60, duration=0.5)
pyautogui.click()

# Wait for the dropdown to appear
time.sleep(0.5)

# Move to the "More tools" option
pyautogui.moveTo(1470, 260, duration=0.5)
pyautogui.click()

# Move to the "Clear browsing data" option
pyautogui.moveTo(1470, 400, duration=0.5)
pyautogui.click()

time.sleep(2)  # Wait for the clear data dialog to appear

# Press 'Enter' to clear data
pyautogui.press('enter')
time.sleep(1)
```

This should help in clearing your browsing data directly.

action:
import pyautogui
import time

# Open a new tab
pyautogui.hotkey('ctrl', 't')
time.sleep(1)

# Navigate to the settings page for clearing browsing data
pyautogui.typewrite('chrome://settings/clearBrowserData\n', interval=0.1)
time.sleep(2)

# Clear browsing data
pyautogui.press('tab', presses=6, interval=0.2)
pyautogui.press('enter')
time.sleep(1)

# Confirm the action if prompted
pyautogui.press('enter')

import pyautogui
import time

# Open a new tab
pyautogui.hotkey('ctrl', 't')
time.sleep(1)

# Directly navigate to the settings for clearing browsing data
pyautogui.typewrite('chrome://settings/clearBrowserData\n', interval=0.1)
time.sleep(2)

# Move focus to the clear data button and press Enter
pyautogui.press('tab', presses=7, interval=0.2)
pyautogui.press('enter')
time.sleep(1)

# Confirm if any additional prompts appear
pyautogui.press('enter')

import pyautogui
import time

# Open the clear browsing data dialog directly
pyautogui.hotkey('ctrl', 'shift', 'delete')
time.sleep(2)

# Confirm the action to clear data by pressing Enter
pyautogui.press('enter')
time.sleep(1)

import pyautogui
import time

# Open a new tab
pyautogui.hotkey('ctrl', 't')
time.sleep(1)

# Type the URL to access the cookies settings directly
pyautogui.typewrite('chrome://settings/cookies\n', interval=0.1)
time.sleep(2)

# Scroll down to clear cookies and site data
pyautogui.press('tab', presses=10, interval=0.2)
pyautogui.press('enter')
time.sleep(1)

# Confirm clearing cookies
pyautogui.press('enter')
